hypothesis: This strategy exploits mean-reversion opportunities at dynamic support/resistance levels using Williams %R as an overbought/oversold filter, with volume confirmation to avoid false signals. The ATR-based risk management ensures position sizing adapts to volatility while maintaining consistent risk-reward profiles across varying market conditions.

Combining Williams %R with volume oscillator and EMA filter creates a robust mean-reversion signal on FRONTUSDC 1h. When price touches support level with elevated volume and Williams %R confirms oversold conditions, it signals a potential reversal. The EMA acts as a trend filter to avoid false signals during strong trends.

This strategy exploits mean-reversion opportunities in FRONTUSDC by identifying oversold conditions via Williams %R and confirming support levels with volume oscillation above the 20-period EMA. The combination of volume confirmation and momentum divergence helps filter false breakouts, while ATR-based risk management ensures dynamic stop-loss and take-profit levels that adapt to volatility changes.

This strategy exploits mean-reversion opportunities in FRONTUSDC by identifying oversold conditions with Williams %R and confirming volume support levels. The EMA filter ensures trend alignment while ATR-based risk management controls position sizing and exit points based on volatility.
